commentaries.author: Clement Of Alexandria
commentaries.content:
I hear also those words of his, "And these things I say, lest any man should beguile you with enticing words, or one should enter in to spoil you.".
But seeking ends in finding, driving out the empty trifling, and approving of the contemplation which confirms our faith. "And this I say, lest any man beguile you with enticing words"

commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content: Do you see that he says, I have therefore said this, that you may not seek it from men. Delude you, he says, with persuasiveness of speech. For what if any does speak, and speak persuasively?

commentaries.author: Methodius of Olympus
commentaries.content: Blotted out the handwriting which was against us.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
